nodes.label: AI as efficient information retrieval tool
nodes.grounding_span: rather than me performing many searches to understand what the rules relate to, I will just use AI to let me know what the rules are

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness for factual queries
nodes.grounding_span: Often I don't verify it against other sources

nodes.label: AI as code generation assistant for non-coders
nodes.grounding_span: I don't know how to code, or to use PowerQuery, so when I asked the AI to create code for me to simply do what I wanted it to do, I found it very effective

nodes.label: AI inauthenticity in communication
nodes.grounding_span: it's very offputting as you can tell it's not authentic

nodes.label: AI capability for project management tasks
nodes.label_negative: Current AI inadequacy for project management
nodes.grounding_span: If for instance JIRA's AI tool becomes a lot better (I think it's terrible at the moment), I will rely much more on it to assist with the project management type activities

nodes.label: AI as synthesis and collaboration tool
nodes.grounding_span: Google's Notebook LM has been the most groundbreaking thing for me... It cut down a huge amount of time, and being able to share it with colleagues has been useful

nodes.label: Efficiency and time saving
nodes.grounding_span: it ended up saving me a lot of time

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: AI is not good enough or accurate enough to trust

nodes.label: Authenticity in communication
nodes.grounding_span: it's very offputting as you can tell it's not authentic

nodes.label: Skill preservation and human uniqueness
nodes.grounding_span: one of my core skills is elicitation and communication skills - something that AI is not able to do at the moment

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for code generation
nodes.grounding_span: I found it very effective. Minor finetuning was required, but it ended up saving me a lot of time

nodes.label: Distrust of AI for data analysis
nodes.grounding_span: AI is not good enough or accurate enough to trust

nodes.label: Dislike of AI-generated messaging
nodes.grounding_span: it's very offputting as you can tell it's not authentic

nodes.label: Openness to future AI use for project management
nodes.grounding_span: If for instance JIRA's AI tool becomes a lot better... I will rely much more on it

nodes.label: Enthusiasm for Notebook LM
nodes.grounding_span: Google's Notebook LM has been the most groundbreaking thing for me

nodes.label: Single-query with follow-up approach
nodes.grounding_span: Usually single question, with follow up questions

nodes.label: Task-dependent trust calibration
nodes.grounding_span: I find that AI is not good enough or accurate enough to trust, and I often have to find ways to export that data out of the UI
